scriptText:
I. PART 1

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a melancholic, high-drama cinematic scene starring @Image 1  as Robert and @Image 2  as Sarah experiencing a quiet, devastating emotional heartbreak.
[[/INTENT]]
[[LOGIC]]
Maintain spatial logic during physical touch and rejection. Track the precise location of the hands, wedding bands, and Sarah's pendant across cuts.
[[/LOGIC]]
[[AESTHETIC]]
Medium: 35mm film, soft organic grain, naturalistic texture
Palette: Pale amber horizon, cold blue-grey sky, dried ochre grass, sage green, dark navy
Lighting: Late dusk, natural low-contrast ambient light with fading horizon warmth
Location: Vast English countryside wildflower field, dry stalks, dead seed heads
Wardrobe (Robert): White linen shirt, dark olive wool jacket, tan chinos, leather bracelet on right wrist, wedding band on left hand
Wardrobe (Sarah): Navy anorak, muted sage-green dress, gold pendant necklace, wedding band on left hand
Sound: Foley and ambient only. No music. Wind through dry grass, crunching footsteps, raw dialogue
[[/AESTHETIC]]
[[OPENING]]
WS, TRACKING BACKWARD head-on at their walking pace. The low horizon sits in the upper third of the frame under a cooling sky. Sarah is frame-left, arms folded tight across her chest, eyes forward. Robert is frame-right, half a step behind her, gazing intensely at her profile.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] WS, TRACKING BACKWARD -> [ACT] Robert glances down at his own hand, then looks back at Sarah. Wind blows hair across Sarah's cheek; she ignores it -> [ENV] Fading amber light on the dry weeds, constant wind hum.
[CAM] MCU, LOCKOFF, eye level on Sarah's profile from her left -> [ACT] Sarah keeps her gaze locked forward, her chin lifting slightly as she speaks -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "When did you find out." -> [ENV] Wind rustling.
[CAM] MCU, HANDHELD, OTS from behind Robert's left shoulder -> [ACT] Robert reaches his right hand across to touch Sarah's left arm sleeve. Sarah instantly pulls her arm away without breaking stride, opening a physical gap. Robert's hand hangs in the empty air -> [AUDIO] Robert: "Sarah, can we just—" -> Sarah: "I asked you when."
[CAM] MCU, LOCKOFF, slight low angle, three-quarter view of Robert -> [ACT] Robert drops his hand back to his side, letting out a long, visible exhale through his nose. His eyes shift to the right, jaw tightening -> [AUDIO] Robert: "...February. The week before Venice."
[CAM] WS, STEADICAM SIDE-TRACKING -> [ACT] Sarah now ahead of Robert. Her arms unfold and her right hand presses flat against the gold pendant at her collarbone. Her stride slows down, her weight hesitating on her next step -> [AUDIO] Footsteps crunching slower on dry soil
[</BRIEF>]

II. PART 2

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create the continuation of @Video 1  a melancholic, high-drama heartbreak scene starring @Image 1  as Robert and @Image 2  as Sarah, continuing directly from the emotional climax of their confrontation.
[[/INTENT]]
[[LOGIC]]
Ensure strict axis of action and geographic consistency in the open field. Keep cuts motivated by psychological beats. Ensure seamless match-on-action of walking paces, posture, and environmental elements.
[[/LOGIC]]
[[CONTINUITY PROTOCOL]]
Pick up directly from the final frame of @Video 1 . Maintain exact matching of visual style, clothing texture, hair physics, and the low-contrast dusk lighting from @Video 1 . Preserve the characters' spatial relationship: Sarah is walking slightly ahead on frame-left, Robert follows on frame-right. Match the steady pacing, ambient sound atmosphere, and the movement of the wind-blown dry weeds. Match the voice. 
[[/CONTINUITY PROTOCOL]]
[[VISUAL BLOCKING]]
The scene opens with Sarah and Robert walking in profile, side-by-side yet emotionally distant. Throughout the scene, the physical blocking mirrors their growing emotional chasm: Sarah abruptly stops and turns to confront Robert. As Sarah delivers her final words, she walks past him, severing the physical tie, leaving Robert static in the center-frame as she walks away to frame-left, the distance between them widening into absolute isolation.
[[/VISUAL BLOCKING]]
[[AESTHETIC]]
Audio: No Music Raw foley only; rustling grass, wind gusts, hollow gravel steps, raspy voice-overs
[[/AESTHETIC]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
Continue from @Video 1 
[CAM] MCU, eye level on Sarah -> [ACT] Sarah abruptly plants her left foot and pivots her body right to face Robert head-on, eyes locking onto him. Robert steps into the right edge of the frame, pulling up inches from her face -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "(sigh) ... You held my hand, on every bridge in that city."
[CAM] MS, HANDHELD, profile view, Robert Frame-Right, Sarah Frame-Left -> [ACT] Sarah's arms hang at her sides, her right hand making a sharp flicking gesture as she speaks. Robert steps half a pace forward, leaning in desperately -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "You laughed at that waiter who spilled the wine." -> Robert: "Because I meant all of it—"
[CAM] MCU, eye level, on Sarah -> [ACT] Sarah's eyes fill with tears, her lower lip trembling as her voice drops to a raw, quiet tone -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "Every day... EVERY DAY! you didn't tell me was a day you let me live in a world that doesn't exist." -> [ENV] Cold howling wind.
[CAM] MCU, OTS HANDHELD, over Sarah's left shoulder looking at Robert -> [ACT] Robert's mouth parts, jaw working silently as he searches her eyes. Sarah slowly drops her chin, breaking eye contact and turning away -> [AUDIO] Breath catching, ambient wind.
[CAM] WS, perpendicular to their path -> [ACT] Robert remains frozen center-frame. Sarah walks past him, she continues walking right-to-left, increasing the physical distance -> [AUDIO] Wind rustling, footsteps fading
[</BRIEF>]

III. PART 3

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Continue the dramatic resolution of @Video 1  to create a heartbreak scene starring @Image 1  as Robert and @Image 2  as Sarah, continuing directly from their heavy silence in the wildflower field.
[[/INTENT]]
[[CONTINUITY PROTOCOL]]
Maintain exact visual continuity from @Video 1  . Use @Audio 1   @Image 3   as additional reference only (voice sound, tone). 
[[/CONTINUITY PROTOCOL]]
[[VISUAL BLOCKING]]
The scene opens with Sarah walking away on frame-left, while Robert walks slightly behind her on frame-right along a dirt path in a wide field. Maintain exact visual continuity of the characters' designs: Robert's white linen shirt, dark olive jacket, and grey curls; Sarah's navy anorak, sage-green dress, and gold pendant. Ensure the cold, diffused dusk lighting, desaturated color palette, and the slow-paced editing of @video1 remain perfectly consistent.
[[/VISUAL BLOCKING]]
[[AESTHETIC]]
Medium: 35mm film, low-contrast, shallow depth of field, naturalistic grain
Palette: Pale amber horizon dusk sky fading to grey-blue overhead, dried ochre field, sage, navy
Lighting: Dimming, soft ambient natural dusk light
Location: Extensive dry wildflower field, swaying seed heads
Sound: No Music, Raw foley only; rustling weeds, deep sighs, vocal cracks, low wind gusts
[[/AESTHETIC]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
Continuation from @Video 1 
[CAM] MCU, on Robert -> [ACT] Robert halts, his brows furrowing as his lower lip catches under his teeth. His chin lifts as he projects his voice toward Sarah off-screen -> [AUDIO] Robert: "... Sarah!"
[CAM] WS, HANDHELD, low angle through foreground dried stalks -> [ACT] Sarah stops mid-stride, facing camera with her back to him. Robert takes a single step forward, his hands opening slightly at his sides, fingers spread. Sarah's shoulders rise with a slow inhale, staying still -> [AUDIO] Robert: "I'm ... telling you now. I'm telling you here..." -> [ENV] Dry grass rustling.
[CAM] CU, LOCKOFF, dead-on framing of Robert -> [ACT] Robert takes a deep breath, his chest rising under his shirt. He looks up at the sky, scans the field, his eyes glossy and wet with unshed tears, before looking back down at Sarah -> [AUDIO] Robert: "...because this is the only place I've ever known how to be afraid."
[CAM] MS, HANDHELD, OTS over Robert's right shoulder -> [ACT] Sarah's shoulder drops. She slowly pivots her head and hips, turning to face him. Her expression is soft, eyes wet, anger completely gone -> [ENV] Wind blowing hair across her cheek.
[CAM] TCU, telephoto two-shot compressing the gap -> [ACT] Robert's eyes search her face. Sarah's expression softening. She gives a micro-nod, her lips parting -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "How long do we have." -> [ACT] Robert slowly closes and opens his eyes, a tremor on his lower lip.
[CAM] WS, SLOW DOLLY-OUT -> [ACT] They stand four steps apart, staring at each other. The camera slowly pulls back, making them smaller as the vast field and cold sky dominate the frame
[</BRIEF>]

cues.selectedText: You laughed at that waiter who spilled the wine.

Sarah's eyes fill with tears, her lower lip trembling as her voice drops to a raw, quiet tone
Cold howling wind.

cues.selectedText: Every day... EVERY DAY! you didn't tell me was a day you let me live in a world that doesn't exist.
MCU, OTS HANDHELD, over Sarah's left shoulder looking at Robert
Robert's mouth parts, jaw working silently as he searches her eyes.
Breath catching, ambient wind.

WS, perpendicular to their path
Robert remains frozen center-frame.
Sarah walks past him, she continues walking right-to-left, increasing the physical distance

MCU, on Robert
Robert halts, his brows furrowing as his lower lip catches under his teeth.
His chin lifts as he projects his voice toward Sarah off-screen

cues.speaker: Robert
cues.selectedText: ... Sarah!
WS, HANDHELD, low angle through foreground dried stalks
Sarah stops mid-stride, facing camera with her back to him.
Dry grass rustling.
Robert takes a single step forward, his hands opening slightly at his sides, fingers spread.
Sarah's shoulders rise with a slow inhale, staying still

cues.speaker: Robert
cues.selectedText: I'm ... telling you now. I'm telling you here...
CU, LOCKOFF, dead-on framing of Robert
Robert takes a deep breath, his chest rising under his shirt.
He looks up at the sky, scans the field, his eyes glossy and wet with unshed tears, before looking back down at Sarah

cues.speaker: Robert
cues.selectedText: ...because this is the only place I've ever known how to be afraid.
MS, HANDHELD, OTS over Robert's right shoulder
Sarah's shoulder drops. She slowly pivots her head and hips, turning to face him.
Wind blowing hair across her cheek.
Her expression is soft, eyes wet, anger completely gone
TCU, telephoto two-shot compressing the gap
Robert's eyes search her face.
Sarah's expression softening. She gives a micro-nod, her lips parting

cues.speaker: Sarah
cues.selectedText: How long do we have.
Robert slowly closes and opens his eyes, a tremor on his lower lip.
WS, SLOW DOLLY-OUT
They stand four steps apart, staring at each other. The camera slowly pulls back, making them smaller as the vast field and cold sky dominate the frame
Wind through dry grass, a single distant bird call
vast field and cold sky
FADE TO BLACK
